# Mark AiConfigModel.md non-authoritative with the ADR-0019 pointer — the OQ1 residual sliver

## Context

Surfaced by the #12456 epic-resolution pass (2026-07-16): the source Discussion's OQ1 criterion required `AiConfigModel.md` to be retired, reduced to a one-line pointer, OR kept as an explicitly NON-AUTHORITATIVE deep-dive under ADR 0019's sole authority. The doc was correctly reduced (48 lines, intent-notes shape, already ConfigProvider-era) — but the surviving file carries **zero** mentions of ADR 0019, "authority", or a non-authoritative marker (grep-verified). A reader landing there today has no signal that the ADR is the read-gate authority — the exact split-brain sliver OQ1 existed to close.

## The Fix

One edit to `learn/agentos/AiConfigModel.md`: a header line immediately under the H1, e.g.

> Non-authoritative intent notes. The authority for every AiConfig pattern, antipattern, and sanctioned shape is `learn/agentos/decisions/0019-aiconfig-reactive-provider-ssot.md` (the §critical_gates read-gate) — if this page and the ADR ever disagree, the ADR wins.

Nothing else changes; the intent prose is good and current.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The doc's header carries the explicit non-authoritative marker + the ADR-0019 pointer.
- [ ] `grep -c "0019" learn/agentos/AiConfigModel.md` ≥ 1.

## Out of Scope

Any content change to the intent notes; the ADR itself.

## Related

Parent: #12456 (the OQ1 row's residual; linked natively) · authority: ADR 0019 · origin criterion: Discussion #12453 OQ1
